---
title: "Upgrading to 20.04 fails immediately"
date: 2020-10-02
forum: Desktop Environments
---

### Post by onesixtyfourth on 2020-10-02
Hi I am trying to update my 18.04 installation to 20.04 following [these instructions]("https://linuxconfig.org/how-to-upgrade-ubuntu-to-20-04-lts-focal-fossa#h3-1-1-upgrading-from-ubuntu-18-04-lts"). After doing all the updating, upgrading and rebooting I consistently get this message when I run the command sudo do-release-upgrade

```
Checking for a new Ubuntu release
Please install all available updates for your release before upgrading.
```

### Post by ajgreeny on 2020-10-02
Just open a terminal and run ```
sudo apt update
sudo apt upgrade
``` to make sure that your 18.04 system is fully updated then try the version upgrade to 20.04 again.

---

### Post by ActionParsnip on 2020-10-02
What is the output of:
```

lsb_release -a; uname -a

```
Thanks

---

### Post by onesixtyfourth on 2020-10-03
Yes I had already done that before I posted here. I think the issue is that I have something installed that is not picked up ny that and blocks the upgrade. I have gone for a clean install now and just getting all my other items reset.
